name: agent-tui
tagline: Drives terminal UI apps through a screenshot/type/press/wait loop for automated testing of CLI/TUI applications, including the Gemini CLI.
verdict: Strong procedural design: explicit observe-decide-act-verify loop, a dedicated failure-recovery table, and an idempotent daemon-restart check. But its own install instructions pipe a GitHub-hosted script straight into `sh` unmitigated, other installs are unpinned/global, tmux is a required but undeclared dependency, and there is no version or changelog.

highlights.summary: Recommended install pipes a remote script directly into sh with no review step.
Alternative installs (npm i -g agent-tui, cargo install --git .../agent-tui.git) are global/unpinned with no version pin.
331-line SKILL.md keeps a philosophy essay, ASCII decision framework, anti-pattern gallery, and quick reference all inline with no split to reference files.
